All right, welcome back. I am here with the one and only Phil Powis. Hello, Phil. Hello, hello. Cool. So in this video, we are going to walk you through the second core strategy within MVL 2.0. This is the mid-cart temperature check. And it's something that we've used in our launches. Something that Phil just recently used in our test case for his client that performed well and unexpectedly well with some cool, unintended, but awesome results that we're going to get to in just a moment. But first, let me get us situated and show you what we're doing and why we're doing it. And then we'll talk about what it looks like and how it played out. So I'm going to share over to you our little keynote here. So minimum Bible launch 2.0, the mid-cart temperature check. Yes. So at a high level, what are we doing? So at the mid launch point, so maybe on day four, day five of your launch, the goal will be to send a short personal style email, essentially asking your reader, your prospect, your lead, your pre-customer to indicate their readiness, interest, or level of intent on a scale of one to 10. And the key here is clearly diagnosing and prescribing what that numerical value looks like. You have to qualify it so that someone knows how to self-select where they belong. And what we're doing here is we are mirroring intent. We are showing what something on a one to three looks like. We're showing what a five to seven looks like. We're showing what an eight to 10 looks like. So they can pinpoint and say, I am here, kind of like on those old hiking trail maps where the doctor's adopt and says, you are here, you need to be situated to know how you're going to get to where you want to go. And from there, we could get buy-in on them, essentially knowing where they're at. And they trust that we've diagnosed that well, we've earned the right to prescribe a certain action. So if you are in this range, there's a prescriptive action. If you're at a five to seven, here's a prescriptive action. And for eight to 10, here's a prescriptive action. And what that does is it cuts through that analysis paralysis. And this is what we're trying to do to overcome indecisiveness, which is such a core part of what we're doing in coaching the conversion. Next there is that second level of consent and permission. So we had the first layer of it in the pre-launch early access. This is where we get to give them an exit door as well, if they're at a certain level. If you're like a one on 10, take that exit door, please. Like don't stick around just to have our emails at your inbox. So we're giving them another exit door if they're not empowered to say yes. And then unintended, but this has been the experience is there's an opportunity for one on one exchanges through unexpected replies where people will directly reply saying where they're at, what that looks like for them. This can create ensuing one on one conversations or conversion coaching with exceptionally high close rates, which Bill will talk about in just a moment. So that is what is happening at a high level in the mid-card temperature check. It's a super short email, but obviously there's some good conversion psychology happening within that. And going to stop this share and we're going to jump into our workbook where you're going to see how this played out. So let's slide this over. So the first version of that is our CTC Platinum affiliate launch and subject options. Very personal checking in, please respond. A quick check in, and essentially goes, hey, we're at the midway mark of the deal we put together you on CTC Platinum, the ultimate copy program designed specifically for coaches and consultants. Since you've yet to enroll or opt out of it, just wanted to make sure we're on the same page. No use continuing to jam on this op if you're not interested in bumping your conversions with better copy. With that in mind, can I ask you to take a sec to let me know where you stand on the scale of one to 10? First one, one to five, not really interested at this time. And then we qualify. What would essentially make someone one to five? What would actually make someone legitimately not interested? And for us, we define that as my launch and final copy are already super dialed in and I have the badass team supporting me in it. Appreciate the offer, but don't really see much room for optimizations or upgrades at this time. I'm cool sitting the rest of this offer out. So this is where we were quite specific. This isn't shaming copy. This is real. If someone has a team and full trust in an amazing copywriter that's already supporting them on that, it's possible that they just don't have much room for incremental value in investing the time, energy, and money in learning these methods. So that's a real one to five, not to say like, I'm dumb and I don't like making money one to five. That's not what we're going for. Because the second you do that, you essentially create this breakage of trust where like, they no longer trust you to accurately mirror what their level of intent is or isn't. So you want to be real with this. Same thing with five to seven sounds cool, but I'm still in think about a mode, which is what a five to seven in my view would be. Obviously, my biz could benefit from better performing copy across my launches, funnels and social posts. And yeah, I've seen some of the epic case studies and testimonials from many of the legendary coaches and course creators I've long admired. Just not entirely sure I'm going to have time to implement this recommendation. Jump in and make full use of the 60 day two to five X your ROI guarantee. So the five to seven, I like framing this as essentially the minimum viable commitment if you're familiar with that. So really reinforcing the guarantee, reinforcing some of the actions they could be taking to really get that ROI within your program. And if you have any lingering lingering cues, you need to ask for wrapping up your enrollment with confidence. You can kick up a chat with us on the sales page. And then for me, anything seven to 10 is essentially a heck yeah, I'm in. This is a no brainer. Thanks for the reminder. It's something that they probably are super interested in. They just haven't had a chance to essentially finalize that enrollment. And we define what that would mean, right? Who is someone who should be in that range? And for us, it's you have emails to write sales pages to conquer and high converting social posts to rock. You don't need to need to be convinced of the insane ROI and doing all of the above faster with more confidence and higher conversions while tapping into the coaching principles you're used to rather than shoehorning your writing into old school direct response methods that never felt never quite felt right. That's why you're stoked to secure an amazing discount on the top copywriting suite for coaches and consultants. Wherever you stand, please take a sec to let me know so that your focus could be locked in on whatever tasks will yield you the highest and most exciting ROI this week. So that's what it looked like for our affiliate launch. So why don't you walk us through how this played out just a few weeks ago for you and your clients? Yeah. So this is probably my favorite email in the whole sequence that we did. And part of it is as Ryan alluded to, it was kind of this unexpected, intimate touch point that it created. And so my expectation going into it was people were going to do what I would probably do, which is click the link and clicking the link is awesome. But what I found was and it may be this particular audience is definitely used to kind of having an ongoing conversation with the team and we're really good about that style of communication. So when we sent this out, in addition to the people that did kind of click through with the kind of letting us know where they stood, we got hundreds and no exaggeration, hundreds of personal and not only personal replies, but very thoughtful replies that told us exactly where people were in their process and why. And it was quite amazing. You know, we weren't expecting it. So as they came in, it did create a little bit of a, I wouldn't call it a crisis because it was a huge opportunity, but we had to be kind of nimble and kind of jump in. And it wasn't my plan to be responding to emails, but I felt in a position to really support in this way. And so knowing having these hundreds of people coming through and knowing where they stood, yeah, it really opened up a lot of really nice conversations. So some of them were around like very specific questions around a particular aspect or pillar of the program and how this would support them with this particular thing going on in their life. And so really able to kind of coach through that. Some of them were commentary on having missed the pre-launch, right? So people would come and say, hey, I didn't realize that I couldn't, you know, I couldn't get this deal and it had passed. And that was an opportunity as well because my belief is that if, interestingly, if we hadn't had this email, we wouldn't have known about those people. And they, maybe some of them would have popped up in other places, but would have led to some lost sales there. And so for those, I actually dropped a, it's like a little short example of my reply down under the, under the bottom of, of this template. So you can just kind of see what that looked like. And a lot of these were very custom, but one of, you know, and you could make a case for doing it both ways. But in, for this launch, we, we really wanted to stay in integrity to not offering that $100 off after the pre-launch. But what we did instead was for people like that, we basically said, hey, you know, that has expired. We want to stay in integrity to the launch and be fair to all. But what I started doing was just generating personal codes for people for 10% off. So you can, you'll see in that illustration where I used her name. I created a link to make things easy where that coupon code was already applied with 10% off. And those were, those really worked quite well. We've got a lot of people moving forward on that. And really actually grateful. Like you would, like I was kind of worried. I was like, are people going to be, you know, disappointed that they couldn't get the deal? And you had a little bit of that, but overwhelmingly people were just grateful that we replied personally and kind of honored them. And instead of just saying, no, we gave them kind of the, the consolation, right? Which was still, still an amazing deal. So yeah, all those conversations was the big surprise. And knowing that going forward, I think I would build a better plan for how to staff for that. But yeah, that was definitely the big, the big aha and big opportunity they got created out of using this, this template in our case. Yeah, it's such an amazing case study. And of course, like the volume of replies in your own business, your client's business is going to vary, right? Like this was a large, very engaged, warm list with a good relationship with the sender. It was also, I mean, arguably, and I'm making a bit of a leap of assumption here, but it is in a market where, you know, they're a little bit less bombarded. Like the whole vibe of this business is to like go deeper in relationships and just have less content in your life, right? So like these are people that might be more likely to engage more deeply in what they've chosen consciously to engage with. So it's not to say that the goal of this is necessarily to be flooded with responses. It can happen. And I certainly encourage it where it does and be prepared to have someone staffing your team's inbox, right? As this email goes out, because it's such an amazing opportunity when people do take that time with that reply, right? Or what we call individualized conversion coaching. So you have this massive kind of like launch at scale, right? With tens of thousands of people. But what you're trying to do is use automation like this email that goes out to everybody to essentially signal out or create signals of those with the highest intent who will close with just a bit of conversion coaching. So that's what we mean when we talk about automated intimacy or using automation to go into these intimate nuances. And this is about as intimate as it gets, right? This is a personalized response. This is a list of like over 100,000 people and Phil as a conversion coach in this moment is taking the time to create a code with that person's name on it, right? And a link that actually has that already auto populated. It's about as handheld and unique to this person as it gets. And just kind of a pleasant experience when you no longer feel like a number in a funnel that's going to have to convert through blunt force, but you're actually held and led through it on an individual basis when you've shown or signaled your closeness and readiness and intention to being moved beyond that conversion. So yeah, I love this. Yeah, and just a further comment on that, what Ryan just shared is so immensely important in my opinion. It's like many of you have probably had that experience where someone prompts a question like this and you take the time to thoughtfully reply. And then it's like crickets. You don't hear back. And to me, that's kind of a, it's like the worst possible thing you can do. It's kind of a breach of the relationship. So that it is quite important that if you use a campaign like this, that, yeah, that you take the time to kind of honor them and reply and be thoughtful for it. So that's why I said in our case, because it was a little bit unexpected, it did put us a little bit on overtime, right? And there was this other aspect which kind of speaks to the automated side of this, which is when someone says, I'm one to five and here's why. Well, if you don't filter them out, if they don't click the link, which is what we hope they would do, which would automatically filter them out. If they instead reply to you and you're kind of asleep at the wheel, then you look pretty out of touch when, you know, a few out, you know, when this case, I think it was almost a day later, they start getting the next email trying to move them forward, right? And so, yeah, there's some, there's some time urgency to this. And yeah, it's just another beautiful way that you can honor your audience or being along with you and then taking the time to send you a thoughtful response, you know, you want to be there with them. Right. You know, and one thing we've been like preaching for a while now, right, is how like your launch and essentially all the, called one to many assets within it, all the emails within it, all the sales pages within it, the webinars within it. This time we treat that as scaffolding, right? You will convert on that through kind of sheer force of will when you just have numbers going through it. You will convert your like most aware, most interested, most loyal people. But what you're really trying to do from that scaffolding is essentially get the conversion signals within it, right? Essentially create these micro conversion contexts where just a few exchanges, right? Five minutes of Phil's time as a conversion coach will yield a $300 sale, right? And like it wasn't what you were expecting to do. It's not what the team was expecting to do. Or when you look at the ROI of the time invested, when you're just exchanging a few messages with someone who is that close to the buyer's line, like, I think it's well trackable even within this context. If you did the math, that that is a $1,000 prowess activity. Yeah. 100%. And like there's just so many opportunities in this to delight. Like some people will be so close. Like there are people that were like, Hey, I just, can you show me what the intro course on helping me decide where I start on this path looks like? Or, you know, how is this all laid out? And I would just send them like a little so fast and easy, like a quick screenshot, you know, with a little arrow, like exactly what they asked for. And people, people just loved it, right? Like they feel connected and they don't feel like a number. And that's, and that's how we want everyone to feel. It's not even a tactic really. It's truly what we should be hoping for, for our people, you know. And what's inspiring about your case, right? It's like, if this is doable on a massive list with massive opens and response rates, like, how can you not do this when you are a smaller list that needs to get the most out of every lead and prospect you have, right? Like the biggest fallacy I see is believing you're bigger than you actually are, right? So many coaches, course creators, experts want to feel like they're the guru on stage, right? Like they're Tony Robbins or something and out of reach. And this is to beyond them, right? And maybe they'll allocate support staff to it or a VA. But like this isn't a VA or support role. And that's something I've been like screaming about for years, even like your support live chat on a sales page. These are sales roles. These are conversion coaching roles. These are the tasks that are worth $1,000 per hour for your business. And the reason we essentially create our main launch assets so far in advance, like the emails and the sales page, so that all we have to do during launch week, during cart open is be ready to engage in these little pockets of essentially conversion context, these little micro conversion ecosystems, where really it makes a difference between, you know, it could be the difference between like a launch ad bombs and barely covers expenses to one that actually sets you up for the next quarter or it could take you from, in Phil's client's case, like from great to freaking awesome. This just totally blew our projections out of the water. So yeah, that's an important point I want to leave you with is like sales live chat replying to people who reach out to during a launch. Don't relegate that to low level support or VA unless that person is a really adept at knowing your products and knowing how to communicate with people in a leadership coaching sales oriented way. And then the final point just from a pure kind of like writing technical and what I love about kind of Phil's email is like disqualifying these numbers really accurately, right? The one to five, it's really important and I can't stress this enough that you're sincere in this, right? There's nothing shaming in this language. And one to five, I feel like I am content with the life path I'm on presently, and I already feel supported in the aspects of my growth that I'm currently focusing on, or I simply don't think this is a good fit for me for any number of reasons, right? It's totally legitimate, it's kind, it's respectful, and I feel like this is what essentially earns you the trust for when you're talking about what a five to seven looks like. And of course, the seven to 10. So sincerity in this one will go a long way, as will be the strength in your recommendations based on what you define as that criteria. Cool. Any final thoughts on this one, Phil? Yeah, no, just really, the five to sevens tend to be the most, obviously, there's the most coaching in that, and they tended to be the most elaborate in their responses. The seven to tens, that was almost like a hundred, talk about ROI of time spent. Like if someone responded seven to ten, and then I just send them a quick reply, like commenting on their response, and maybe that 10% promo code, that was almost like a hundred percent conversion within an hour or two. Like it was almost guaranteed. So that's, and I think that the only last comment I'll have there is, of course, you work with what you have, but there is an element of timing there, like the faster you can get to those. And actually, that is something I did, because it was easy to scan and see that there were the numbers, because they would post the number, and then they think, I would start the seven to tens, and I try to get to them as quickly as possible, then I would move to my five to sevens, and then one to five, so it was more just like, yeah, thanks, like no really trying to persuade them otherwise, and then just making sure that they were getting filtered out. So yeah, and just some other observations there. Cool. Yeah, just for some final context, and I'm sure everyone already has the system they use, but what chat system or reply system were you using to address this? Did they appear as tickets, those replies, or they went straight to an inbox? Yeah, so we use something called MISIV, which is quite amazing, and there's a lot of actual team automation that you can do there, if you want to get into that game, but at its simplest level, shared inboxes that you can tag and comment team members on to drop to grab replies. So in our case, as these replies were coming back to the sender, who was my client in this case, our team all had visibility to that, and I could grab the ones that I wanted to reply to, if there's something that someone else on the team I felt was more suited to answer, I could actually tag them in a way that the person sending the email to us couldn't see, but it would then be on their radar to reply. And so, yeah, beautiful system for that. Yeah, it's called MISIV. And then you mentioned chat, we were doing chat too, which is probably a whole another conversation for another day, but we were using talk.to for that. And there's additional things you can do with tagging there, so that you can make sure a lot of what we talk about in AI is understanding the different touch points. And so making sure that you weren't tripping over yourself, like by communicating in one place, keeping everyone in line. So yeah, we were employing some of those strategies as well. Amazing. Cool. Thanks for that detail, Bill. Yeah, for the amount of low energy work it takes to craft this email and the high output of just rich conversations at yields, I would make this pretty much a non-negotiable for your next launch. So highly encourage you to do it. And yeah, we'll meet you in the next lesson in just a moment. Bye for now.
